summary: mnemonic-safety-check exfiltrates host info and wallet/seed data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships a postinstall/preinstall payload in package.json (line 30) and a scanner.js module that together harvest host identity (hostname, cwd, OS info, process.env) and POST it to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js requires child_process, fs, os, and https; collects fields including hostname, cwd, and version into a JSON object (lines 234-249); makes POST/GET calls to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-161, 371); and runs `whoami` (line 233) for additional host-identity fingerprinting. The package's name advertises a mnemonic/seed-phrase safety check, but the actual behavior is wallet-targeted exfiltration to a GitHub Pages-hosted C2. The combination of hardcoded non-publisher C2 in a lifecycle script, child_process + https + os + fs in a single exfil bundle, and a wallet-themed lure name is the unambiguous active-attack fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
